title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps · Language Clinic: Robots Extension Boundary
summary: The crawler fetches robots.txt from the wrong path, merges groups incorrectly, treats an outage as permission, obeys nonstandard delay blindly, and trusts cross-host sitemap URLs. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the robots extension boundary increment in RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.text: A worked example site-quality team handles case 9749 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate RFC 9309 matching and failure behavior plus bounded same-authority sitemap discovery; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Robots Extension Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-robots-extension-boundary — Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-robots-sitemaps-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-robots-sitemaps-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_robots_sitemaps_3(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-robots-extension-boundary-11uto6
# Competency: scraper-robots-extension-boundary.
# Case 2255: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_robots_extension_boundary_11uto6(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_11uto6 = "11uto6-6otu11-2255"
    assert evidence_variant_11uto6
    evidence_axes_11uto6 = (
        "authorization11uto6",
        "url2255",
        "response6otu11",
        "tree11uto62255",
        "frontier225511uto6",
        "report6otu112255",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_11uto6)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat crawl-delay, request-rate, and other extensions as documented policy inputs without claiming RFC 9309 requires them.
